Package Dungeon_Classes

Class DestructibleTile

java.lang.Object
Dungeon_Classes.Tile
Dungeon_Classes.DestructibleTile
public class DestructibleTile extends Tile
Represents a tile within the dungeon layout that can be broken or collected. Manages specialized behaviors for dynamic map objects including spawning treasure rewards, dropping randomized currency amounts, and granting items.
Author:
Katigbak and Porciuncula

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class Dungeon_Classes.Tile

    damage, destructible, passable, symbol, x, y

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DestructibleTile(int x, int y, char symbol, int goldDrop, Item itemDrop, boolean treasure)
    Constructs a destructible tile with direct assignments for drops and attributes.
    DestructibleTile(Tile parentTile)
    Constructs a destructible tile copy using attributes from an existing parent Tile instance.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    assignProperties()
    Overrides state definitions to assign specialized parameters like randomized currency scaling or default consumable items based on the active symbol signature.
    void
    dropTreasure()
    Randomizes the treasure tile's content state, transforming its layout representation into either a raw item drop ('I') or a gold drop ('g').
    int
    getGoldDrop()
    Retrieves the currency amount dropped upon destruction.
    Item
    getItemDrop()
    Retrieves the item payload dropped upon destruction.
    boolean
    getTreasure()
    Checks if the tile contains treasure contents.
    void
    setGoldDrop(int goldDrop)
    Updates the gold drop value for this tile.
    void
    setItemDrop(Item itemDrop)
    Updates the item drop configuration for this tile.
    void
    setTreasure(boolean treasure)
    Updates the treasure status flag for this tile.

  • Constructor Details

    • DestructibleTile

      public DestructibleTile(int x, int y, char symbol, int goldDrop, Item itemDrop, boolean treasure)
      Constructs a destructible tile with direct assignments for drops and attributes.
      Parameters:
      x - the map column coordinate
      y - the map row coordinate
      symbol - the character representation of the tile
      goldDrop - the exact currency amount contained
      itemDrop - the item payload contained
      treasure - flag specifying if it is a chest configuration

    • DestructibleTile

      public DestructibleTile(Tile parentTile)
      Constructs a destructible tile copy using attributes from an existing parent Tile instance.
      Parameters:
      parentTile - the source tile to copy properties from

  • Method Details

    • getTreasure

      public boolean getTreasure()
      Checks if the tile contains treasure contents.
      Returns:
      true if the tile contains treasure, false otherwise

    • setTreasure

      public void setTreasure(boolean treasure)
      Updates the treasure status flag for this tile.
      Parameters:
      treasure - the new treasure status flag

    • getGoldDrop

      public int getGoldDrop()
      Retrieves the currency amount dropped upon destruction.
      Returns:
      the currency amount dropped upon destruction

    • setGoldDrop

      public void setGoldDrop(int goldDrop)
      Updates the gold drop value for this tile.
      Parameters:
      goldDrop - the new gold drop value

    • getItemDrop

      public Item getItemDrop()
      Retrieves the item payload dropped upon destruction.
      Returns:
      the item payload dropped upon destruction

    • setItemDrop

      public void setItemDrop(Item itemDrop)
      Updates the item drop configuration for this tile.
      Parameters:
      itemDrop - the new item drop configuration

    • dropTreasure

      public void dropTreasure()
      Randomizes the treasure tile's content state, transforming its layout representation into either a raw item drop ('I') or a gold drop ('g').

    • assignProperties

      public void assignProperties()
      Overrides state definitions to assign specialized parameters like randomized currency scaling or default consumable items based on the active symbol signature.
      Overrides:
      assignProperties in class Tile
